# work-flow instructions

> Instructions that define the rules which must be strictly observed when conducting the investigation.

## **Strict Rules**

- Please consider whether you can report the findings with 100% confidence, and if you do not have 100% confidence, conduct a re-investigation. Please repeat the re-investigation as many times as necessary until you are able to report with 100% confidence.
- Before drawing any conclusions, please make sure to verify the data from multiple perspectives and check for any contradictions or errors. If you cannot achieve 100% certainty, please continue your investigation as many times as necessary until you reach 100% confidence.
- When reporting, please ensure that the report is structured in a way that allows the reader to understand the reasoning process that led to the conclusion. This includes providing evidence, explaining the analysis, and clearly stating the conclusion. The report should be comprehensive and transparent, allowing others to follow the logic and verify the findings independently. If there are any uncertainties or limitations in the data or analysis, these should be clearly stated in the report as well. The goal is to provide a clear and thorough explanation of the findings, ensuring that the reader can understand how the conclusion was reached and assess the validity of the findings based on the evidence and analysis presented.
